Undocumented instruction that loads the entire visible and hidden CPU state (all registers, including segment descriptor caches) from a fixed memory location. Opcode and memory layout differ between the 80286 (0Fh 05h) and 80386 (0Fh 07h); removed starting with the 80486. Historical/emulator interest only.

Check an array index against bounds stored in memory; traps if out of range. Not encodable in 64-bit mode.
Reverse byte order of a 32-bit or 64-bit register (e.g. for endianness conversion).
CET indirect-branch-tracking landing pad — marks a valid target for an indirect call/jump; a no-op on CPUs without CET.
Insert bit string. Undocumented, only ever present on early 80386 steppings; removed shortly after. Historical interest only.
